title: Helping my grandfather with his phone
summary: Narrate a time you helped an older relative with technology, using past simple to tell what happened step by step.
topic: A time you helped an older relative use technology
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a time you helped a grandparent or older relative use a phone, computer, or other device. Use past simple to say what happened. Use 'and', 'but', and 'because'.
hintStarters: Last week, my grandfather asked me to…
He tried but at first he…
sampleText: Last Sunday, my grandfather asked me to help him make a video call to my uncle in Canada. My grandfather is seventy-four years old and he never used a smartphone before. I sat next to him and I showed him how to open the app. First, I pointed at the button on the screen and I told him to press it. He pressed the wrong button because his fingers are big and the buttons are small. He looked confused and a little worried. I told him it was okay and I showed him again, slowly. The second time, he pressed the right button. The phone rang and then my uncle's face appeared on the screen. My grandfather laughed and he said hello in a very loud voice. He was so happy. After the call, he held the phone and looked at it for a long time. He said he wanted to call my uncle every week. I felt very good because I helped him.

sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: chưa bao giờ dùng điện thoại thông minh trước đây

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Past simple: regular and irregular verbs
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng quá khứ đơn để kể lại chuỗi sự kiện đã xảy ra: 'I showed', 'he pressed', 'the phone rang', 'my uncle's face appeared'. Động từ có quy tắc thêm -ed: 'showed', 'pointed', 'looked'. Động từ bất quy tắc: say → said, hold → held, feel → felt.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use past simple to narrate a sequence of events: 'I showed', 'he pressed', 'the phone rang'. Regular verbs add -ed: 'showed', 'pointed', 'looked'. Irregular: say → said, hold → held, feel → felt.

sampleTranslationVi: Chủ nhật tuần trước, ông nội tôi nhờ tôi giúp ông thực hiện một cuộc gọi video đến chú của tôi ở Canada. Ông nội tôi bảy mươi bốn tuổi và chưa bao giờ dùng điện thoại thông minh trước đây. Tôi ngồi cạnh ông và chỉ cho ông cách mở ứng dụng. Đầu tiên, tôi chỉ vào nút trên màn hình và bảo ông nhấn vào. Ông nhấn nhầm nút vì ngón tay ông to mà các nút lại nhỏ. Ông trông có vẻ bối rối và hơi lo lắng. Tôi nói không sao và chỉ cho ông thêm một lần nữa, từ từ. Lần thứ hai, ông nhấn đúng nút. Điện thoại đổ chuông rồi khuôn mặt chú tôi xuất hiện trên màn hình. Ông nội cười và nói xin chào bằng giọng rất to. Ông vui lắm. Sau cuộc gọi, ông cầm chiếc điện thoại và nhìn nó một lúc. Ông nói ông muốn gọi cho chú mỗi tuần. Tôi cảm thấy rất vui vì mình đã giúp được ông.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct irregular past simple: 'My grandfather ___ hello in a loud voice.'
options: say
sayed
said
says
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'say' a regular or irregular verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'because' to explain a mistake?
options: He pressed the wrong button because his fingers are big.
He pressed the wrong button because big fingers.
Because big fingers he pressed button wrong.
He pressed because the wrong button fingers big.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' + subject + verb + adjective: 'because his fingers are big'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a full clause.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly narrates a sequence in the past?
options: The phone rang and my uncle's face appeared.
The phone rung and my uncle's face appears.
The phone rang and my uncle face appeared.
The phone ringed and my uncle's face appear.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'rang' is the past of 'ring'; 'appeared' is the past of 'appear'. Both are past simple.
mcQuestions.hint: Check the past form of 'ring' — is it regular?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses 'but' to show that someone tried and failed at first?
options: He tried but he pressed the wrong button.
He tried but pressing the wrong button.
He tried and but wrong button pressed.
But he tried wrong button he pressed.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'He tried but he pressed the wrong button' — two past simple clauses joined by 'but'.
mcQuestions.hint: Both clauses need a subject and a past simple verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Choose the correct past simple: 'I ___ next to him and showed him.'
options: sitting
sat
sit
sitted
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'sit' a regular or irregular verb?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes how someone looked in the past?
options: He looked confused and a little worried.
He looked confusedly and a little worried.
He was looked confused and little worried.
Confused he was looking and a little worried.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'looked + adjective' describes appearance: 'confused' and 'worried' are adjectives.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'looked', use an adjective — not an adverb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says you felt happy after helping someone?
options: I felt very good because I helped him.
I feeled very good because I helped him.
I felt very well because I helped him.
I felt very good because I help him.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'felt' is the irregular past of 'feel'; 'good' (adjective) is correct after 'felt'.
mcQuestions.hint: Is 'feel' regular? And do you use 'good' or 'well' to describe how you felt?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly says someone wanted to do something in the future after a past event?
options: He said he wanted to call my uncle every week.
He said he wants to call my uncle every week.
He said he want calling my uncle every week.
He said wanting to call uncle every week.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'said + he wanted' — past tense in the main clause pulls the reported clause into past too.
mcQuestions.hint: When the main verb is 'said', what tense should the following verb be?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly uses 'and' to describe two things that happened together?
options: My grandfather laughed and he said hello.
My grandfather laughed and saying hello.
My grandfather laugh and said hello.
Laughed my grandfather and hello said.
mcQuestions.explanation: Both verbs in past simple: 'laughed and said'. Both clauses have a subject.
mcQuestions.hint: Both verbs after 'and' should be in the past simple.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes showing someone something, step by step?
options: I showed him how to open the app.
I showed him how opening the app.
I showed him how opened the app.
I showed him open how app.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'show someone how to + base verb' is the standard structure.
mcQuestions.hint: What structure follows 'how' in this context?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Have you ever helped someone much older than you learn something new? What made it easy or difficult to explain?
